title: Create domain-layer unit tests: Email value object and DomainError hierarchy
description: Zero domain-layer tests exist. The Email value object is the only concrete runtime logic in the domain layer and has no test coverage. Create: backend/tests/unit/domain/value-objects/email.value-object.test.ts (valid construction, uppercase normalisation, whitespace trimming, each invalid format, instanceof InvalidEmailError on throw, 254-char boundary) and backend/tests/unit/domain/errors/domain.error.test.ts (DomainError constructor, InvalidEmailError constructor, full instanceof chain: InvalidEmailError instanceof DomainError instanceof Error, catch-as-base-class). Also add max-length guard to Email constructor: if (normalised.length > 254) throw new InvalidEmailError(raw).
close_reason: Fixed in commit 1242b10. Adversarial review PASS, 55 tests pass, tsc clean.

title: Fix race condition: replace updateFailedLoginAttempts with atomic increment
description: IUserRepository.updateFailedLoginAttempts(userId, count, lockedUntil) takes an absolute count, requiring the use case to read-increment-write in application memory. Two parallel failed login requests will both read count=3, both write count=4, recording 4 failures instead of 5 — allowing an attacker to bypass the 5-attempt lockout (FR-008) via concurrent requests. Fix: replace with incrementFailedLoginAttempts(userId: string, lockThreshold: number, lockDurationMs: number): Promise<{ failedLoginAttempts: number; lockedUntil: Date | null }> which pushes the atomic DB-level increment and lockout threshold logic into the repository contract.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 1242b10. Adversarial review PASS, 55 tests pass, tsc clean.

title: Add missing domain error subclasses for all auth failure cases
description: Only InvalidEmailError exists. The auth controller maps use-case domain errors to HTTP codes defined in contracts/auth.md, but the following typed errors are absent: InvalidCredentialsError (→ 401), AccountLockedError (→ 403), AccountDeactivatedError (→ 403), InvalidRefreshTokenError (→ 401), InvalidResetTokenError (→ 400), SamePasswordError (→ 422). Without these, the presentation layer cannot reliably catch and map use-case outcomes — developers would resort to string matching or raw Error instances, losing type safety at the boundary. Add all 6 to backend/src/domain/errors/domain.error.ts and export from domain/index.ts.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 1242b10. Adversarial review PASS, 55 tests pass, tsc clean.

title: Add AuthEventLogEntity and IAuthEventLogRepository to domain layer
description: FR-017 requires audit logging on every auth event (LOGIN_SUCCESS, LOGIN_FAILURE, ACCOUNT_LOCKED, LOGOUT, TOKEN_REFRESH, PASSWORD_RESET_REQUEST, PASSWORD_RESET_SUCCESS, PASSWORD_CHANGED). The AuthEvent enum exists in domain/enums but there is no AuthEventLogEntity interface or IAuthEventLogRepository contract. Every use case needs to inject IAuthEventLogRepository, making this a hard blocker for all 5 use cases. Add: backend/src/domain/entities/auth-event-log.entity.ts (id, userId: string|null, event: AuthEvent, ipAddress: string|null, userAgent: string|null, createdAt) and backend/src/domain/repositories/auth-event-log.repository.interface.ts (create method only — append-only log). Export both from domain/index.ts.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 1242b10. Adversarial review PASS, 55 tests pass, tsc clean.

title: Write security tests: user enumeration prevention
description: FR-009 and FR-007 both require the API never reveals whether a given email is registered. Test three surfaces: (1) POST /auth/login — 401 response body for unknown email is byte-for-byte identical to 401 for wrong password on known email, response time difference under 50ms (bcrypt dummy comparison must always run); (2) POST /auth/forgot-password — 200 response body for unregistered email is byte-for-byte identical to 200 for registered email, email service NOT invoked for unregistered case; (3) POST /auth/reset-password with fabricated token returns 400 INVALID_RESET_TOKEN without disclosing whether token prefix matches any userId. Directly validates SC-005 and User Story 4 anti-enumeration scenario.

title: Write security tests: brute-force lockout sequence
description: SC-006 requires lockout on the 5th consecutive failure with no further logins permitted during the lockout window. Test the full sequence at the contract level (Supertest + mocked repository): four failures return 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS; fifth failure returns 403 ACCOUNT_LOCKED (exact error code 'ACCOUNT_LOCKED', no remaining-time leakage); sixth attempt while locked still returns 403 without calling bcrypt.compare; after simulating lockedUntil expiry, correct password succeeds. Also assert response times for failed attempts do not diverge enough to enable a timing oracle (bcrypt always awaited, not short-circuited on unknown email).

title: Write security tests: refresh token replay attack
description: FR-004 mandates that a refresh token is invalidated immediately upon use. A stolen token reused after rotation must be rejected. Tests must cover: use a refresh token once -> receive new token pair, old token is revoked; replay the original token -> 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN (token reuse detection); replay the rotated token after a second rotation -> 401; present a refresh token that belongs to a different user's tokenHash -> 401 (cross-user isolation). Assert that on reuse detection the system revokes all tokens for that user if that policy is implemented, or document the gap as a separate bead.

title: Write contract tests for auth routes via Supertest
description: Contract tests validate the full HTTP surface against the shapes defined in specs/001-user-auth/contracts/auth.md using createApp() without a real DB (repositories mocked at the DI boundary). Each endpoint must assert status code, Content-Type, and the exact { data, meta, error } envelope shape. Coverage per route: login — 200 with token pair, 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS, 403 ACCOUNT_LOCKED, 403 ACCOUNT_DEACTIVATED, 422 VALIDATION_ERROR, 429 RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED; refresh — 200, 401, 422, 429; logout — 204, 401 UNAUTHORIZED, 422; forgot-password — 200 identical envelope for registered and unregistered email, 422, 429; reset-password — 200, 400 INVALID_RESET_TOKEN, 422. Assert no passwordHash or internal stack trace appears in any response body.

title: Write unit tests for RefreshTokenUseCase
description: RefreshTokenUseCase implements rolling token rotation and is the primary defence against token-replay attacks. Tests must cover: valid token exchanges for new accessToken + new refreshToken, old token is revoked atomically (FR-003, FR-004); already-revoked token returns INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN (FR-004 replay guard); expired token returns INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN; token belonging to a different userId rejected (cross-user replay); successful rotation appends TOKEN_REFRESH to AuthEventLog (FR-017); missing or empty refreshToken body field returns a validation error before reaching use-case logic. All repository and JWT service calls mocked.

title: Write unit tests for LoginUseCase
description: LoginUseCase contains the most complex branching logic and is the entry gate for all roles. Tests must cover: valid credentials issue accessToken + refreshToken pair with correct TTLs (FR-001, FR-002); wrong password returns generic error without leaking which field failed (FR-007, SC-005); 5th consecutive failure sets lockedUntil = now+15min and returns ACCOUNT_LOCKED (FR-008, SC-006); locked account with lockedUntil in the future rejected without checking password; lockedUntil in the past auto-clears lockout; DEACTIVATED account returns ACCOUNT_DEACTIVATED regardless of correct credentials; successful login resets failedLoginAttempts to 0; LOGIN_SUCCESS and LOGIN_FAILURE events appended to AuthEventLog (FR-017). All Prisma calls and JWT/bcrypt services mocked.
close_reason: All required scenarios covered in tests/unit/use-cases/login.use-case.test.ts (committed 5145447 as part of crm-s6k): valid credentials, wrong password generic error, 5th failure lockout, future lockedUntil rejection, expired lock auto-clear, deactivated account, failedLoginAttempts reset on success, LOGIN_SUCCESS/LOGIN_FAILURE audit events. All Prisma and service calls mocked.

title: Configure Jest coverage reporting and enforce 80% threshold gate
description: jest.config.ts already sets coverageThreshold at 80% for branches/functions/lines/statements and collectCoverageFrom targets src/**/*.ts. Verify 'npm test' exits non-zero when coverage drops below threshold. Add a 'test:ci' script alias with --ci --forceExit flags to prevent hanging test runs in CI. Confirm coverage/lcov.info artefact is generated for upload to a coverage service. Add coverage/.gitignore so the generated report is not committed. This is a gate — no auth implementation can be merged if any threshold is violated.
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS (5/5). Committed 4ad67b3.

title: Configure Jest + Supertest test infrastructure for auth feature
description: The existing jest.config.ts and health.routes.test.ts provide the baseline, but the auth test tree does not yet exist. Create the directory scaffold under backend/tests/unit/use-cases/, backend/tests/integration/repositories/, and backend/tests/contract/. Add a shared test-helpers module with mock factory functions for User, RefreshToken, PasswordResetToken, and AuthEventLog Prisma records. Verify that running 'npm test' discovers all new files. This unblocks every downstream test task and ensures factories are shared rather than duplicated per file.
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS (13/13). Committed c640b87.

title: Implement LoginUseCase with account lockout, rolling refresh token, and auth event logging
description: Core authentication flow that all users hit on every session start (P0/critical — FR-001 through FR-008). Validate email/password with bcrypt; reject DEACTIVATED accounts (403 ACCOUNT_DEACTIVATED); reject locked accounts where lockedUntil > now (403 ACCOUNT_LOCKED); on wrong password increment failedLoginAttempts and lock for 15 min on the 5th failure; on success reset counter, issue 15-min JWT access token + opaque refresh token (stored as SHA-256 hash with 7-day TTL), and write LOGIN_SUCCESS or LOGIN_FAILURE + ACCOUNT_LOCKED to AuthEventLog. Return token pair in the contract envelope.
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS (13/13). Committed 5145447.

title: Implement Prisma repository adapters for auth
description: Wire the domain repository interfaces to PostgreSQL via Prisma 5. PrismaUserRepository: findByEmail (lowercase normalised), updateFailedAttempts (atomic increment), lock (set lockedUntil), resetLock, updatePassword, updateStatus. PrismaAuthTokenRepository: create (stores SHA-256 hash), findByHash, revoke (set revokedAt), revokeAllForUser. PrismaPasswordResetRepository: create (1-hour TTL), findByHash, invalidatePrior (delete all prior unused tokens for userId), markUsed. PrismaClient must be injected via constructor — no global singleton import outside infrastructure layer. No raw SQL.
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS (18/18). Committed 1ab649a.

title: Implement infrastructure services: JwtService, BcryptService, NodemailerEmailService
description: Provide the cryptographic and communication primitives that every use case depends on. JwtService: wraps jsonwebtoken to sign 15-min access tokens (JWT_SECRET) and 7-day refresh tokens (REFRESH_TOKEN_SECRET) with typed payloads; no 'any'. BcryptService: wraps bcryptjs with cost factor >= 12 read from env for hash and compare. NodemailerEmailService: sends password-reset request emails and reset-confirmation emails using nodemailer with SMTP_* env config. All three expose narrow interfaces used for constructor DI in use cases.
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS (20/20). Committed 2337aa2.

title: Implement auth event logging infrastructure
description: Provides the append-only audit trail required by every use case (FR-017). Create IAuthEventLogRepository interface (create method only — no update or delete). Implement PrismaAuthEventLogRepository that inserts AuthEventLog rows without exposing PII in application-layer logs (IP address masked to first two octets in log output, full value stored in DB). Wire as a dependency into all five use cases via constructor injection. Log events: LOGIN_SUCCESS, LOGIN_FAILURE, ACCOUNT_LOCKED, TOKEN_REFRESH, LOGOUT, PASSWORD_RESET_REQUEST, PASSWORD_RESET_SUCCESS, PASSWORD_CHANGED.
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS (18/18). Committed 1ab649a.

title: Implement domain layer: entities, value objects, and repository interfaces
description: Establish the innermost Clean Architecture ring so all outer layers can depend on stable contracts. Create: user.entity.ts (with id, email, passwordHash, role, status, failedLoginAttempts, lockedUntil fields — no Prisma imports), email.value-object.ts (immutable, normalises to lowercase + trimmed, throws domain error on invalid RFC 5321 address), and three repository interfaces: IUserRepository, IAuthTokenRepository, IPasswordResetRepository with method signatures matching all use-case queries. No Prisma imports allowed in this layer.
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS. Committed d75d99a.

title: Create Prisma migration for auth models
description: The schema.prisma already defines User, RefreshToken, PasswordResetToken, and AuthEventLog with all required indexes and cascade rules, but no migration has been generated yet. Run 'prisma migrate dev --name init-auth' to produce the SQL migration file under backend/prisma/migrations/ and verify the generated SQL matches the data-model spec (tokenHash unique indexes, cascade deletes, nullable userId on AuthEventLog). This migration is a hard prerequisite for every infrastructure repository task and all integration tests.
close_reason: Migration 20260616161007_init_auth applied to crm_dev. All 4 auth tables + indexes verified. Committed e92d15c.

title: Specify and assign owner for the Admin account-unlock flow
description: FR-008 states that a locked account can be unlocked manually by an Administrator, and the Assumptions section confirms this. However, there is no endpoint contract, no functional requirement covering the Admin action itself, and no acceptance scenario for it. The PM must decide whether Admin unlock is in scope for this module (requiring a new FR and contract) or is deferred to an Admin Management module (requiring an explicit out-of-scope statement and a dependency note).

title: Resolve open edge cases into requirements or explicit out-of-scope decisions
description: Five edge cases are listed in spec.md but remain unresolved: (1) inactivity clock behavior while a user reads without making requests, (2) account deactivation while a session is active, (3) refresh token belonging to a different user's session, (4) reset form submission after link expiry but before browser refresh, and (5) same-password set during reset (partially covered by FR-014 but no acceptance scenario exists). Each must be either converted into a numbered FR with an acceptance scenario or explicitly documented as out-of-scope with a rationale.

title: WU7: Presentation layer — controllers, routes, require-role middleware, contract tests, app wiring
description: Wire the full HTTP presentation layer for 002. Files to create: (1) backend/src/presentation/http/middleware/require-role.middleware.ts — reads req.user (set by authenticate middleware from 001) and checks caller has one of the required roles; returns 403 FORBIDDEN otherwise. (2) backend/src/presentation/http/controllers/customers.controller.ts — maps HTTP req/res to all 8 customer use cases; error-to-HTTP mapping (ForbiddenError→403, DuplicateEmailError→409, NotFoundError→404, ValidationError→422). (3) backend/src/presentation/http/controllers/organizations.controller.ts — maps to 6 org use cases. (4) backend/src/presentation/http/routes/customers.routes.ts — GET/POST /api/v1/customers, GET/PATCH /api/v1/customers/:id, POST /api/v1/customers/:id/deactivate, POST /api/v1/customers/:id/reactivate, GET /api/v1/customers/search, PATCH /api/v1/customers/me. (5) backend/src/presentation/http/routes/organizations.routes.ts — GET/POST /api/v1/organizations, GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/organizations/:id, POST /api/v1/organizations/:id/members, DELETE /api/v1/organizations/:id/members/:customerId. (6) Contract tests: backend/tests/contract/customers.routes.test.ts and organizations.routes.test.ts (mock use cases, test HTTP status codes and response shapes). (7) Update backend/src/app.ts to mount both routers. Also add require-role.middleware.test.ts unit test.
acceptance_criteria: All routes return correct HTTP status codes per contracts/customers.md and contracts/organizations.md. Contract tests cover happy path + error cases. app.ts mounts both routers.

title: WU5: Customer use cases — update, update-own-profile, deactivate, reactivate
description: Implement 4 remaining customer use cases + unit tests. (1) UpdateCustomerUseCase: Admin/Manager; updates fullName/phone/jobTitle/organizationId; Admin-only: email update (triggers re-verification email via invitation service); writes per-field audit entries for each changed field in a Prisma transaction with the update. (2) UpdateOwnProfileUseCase: Customer role only; updates own fullName/phone/jobTitle only (org/email/status/role fields ignored even if sent); writes audit entries. (3) DeactivateCustomerUseCase: Admin only; sets User.status = DEACTIVATED; invalidates all auth tokens via IAuthTokenRepository.revokeAllForUser; writes audit entry. (4) ReactivateCustomerUseCase: Admin only; sets User.status = ACTIVE; writes audit entry. All throw ForbiddenError when caller lacks required role. Tests in backend/tests/unit/use-cases/customers/.
acceptance_criteria: Audit entries written atomically with update. Deactivate revokes all tokens via IAuthTokenRepository. Role guards tested for each use case.
close_reason: All 8 customer use cases implemented with unit tests. 31/31 test suites pass, 312 tests. tsc clean. RBAC enforced in every use case. Audit trail written atomically in UpdateCustomer/UpdateOwnProfile. DeactivateCustomer revokes all tokens.

title: WU6: Organization use cases — create, get, list, update, delete, manage-members
description: Implement 6 organization use cases + unit tests. (1) CreateOrganizationUseCase: Admin/Manager; validates name uniqueness (case-insensitive); creates record. (2) GetOrganizationUseCase: Admin/Manager/Agent; returns org details + member list (paginated) + aggregated ticket summary (sum across all members from ITicketSummaryService). (3) ListOrganizationsUseCase: Admin/Manager/Agent; paginated, sorted by name/createdAt. (4) UpdateOrganizationUseCase: Admin/Manager; updates name/emailDomain/industry/primaryContactId; validates uniqueness on name change; primaryContactId must reference an existing Customer. (5) DeleteOrganizationUseCase: Admin only; blocked if org has any members (throws OrganizationHasMembersError). (6) ManageOrganizationMembersUseCase: Admin/Manager; adds or removes a Customer from the org (Customer.organizationId); a customer can belong to at most one org — adding to a second org first removes from the old one. Tests in backend/tests/unit/use-cases/organizations/.
acceptance_criteria: Delete blocked when members exist. One-org-per-customer invariant enforced. Aggregated ticket summary tested with stub.
close_reason: All 6 org use cases implemented with tests: create (name uniqueness + primaryContact validation), get (ticket summary), list (pageSize capped), update (name uniqueness + primaryContact), delete (member guard), manage-members (add/remove with one-org invariant + audit trail). 37/37 suites, 363 tests pass.

title: WU3: Infrastructure — Prisma repositories + service stubs
description: Implement the infrastructure layer for 002. Files: (1) backend/src/infrastructure/repositories/prisma-customer.repository.ts — implements ICustomerRepository with methods: create, findById, findByUserId, findByEmail, list (paginated, filtered), search (ILIKE/GiST), update, updateStatus. (2) backend/src/infrastructure/repositories/prisma-organization.repository.ts — implements IOrganizationRepository with: create, findById, findByName, list, update, delete, addMember, removeMember. (3) backend/src/infrastructure/services/stub-ticket-summary.service.ts — stub implementing ITicketSummaryService returning zeros (activates when 003-ticket-management ships). (4) backend/src/infrastructure/services/auth-user-invitation.service.ts — implements IUserInvitationService by triggering password reset flow via the auth module's PasswordResetRepository. Unit tests for repositories using mocked Prisma client (same pattern as prisma-user.repository.test.ts).
acceptance_criteria: All repository methods tested. No raw SQL (Prisma only). No N+1 queries.
close_reason: Implemented PrismaCustomerRepository (create/find/list/search/updateWithAudit/updateStatus), PrismaOrganizationRepository (create/find/list/update/delete/memberCount), StubTicketSummaryService, AuthUserInvitationService. Added createCustomerEntity/createOrganizationEntity to factories. 23/23 test suites, 242 tests pass. tsc clean.

title: WU4: Customer use cases — create, get, list, search
description: Implement 4 customer use cases + unit tests. All enforce RBAC at use-case layer using the caller's Role from AccessTokenPayload. (1) CreateCustomerUseCase: Admin/Manager only; validates email uniqueness via repo; creates Customer + User records in one transaction; triggers IUserInvitationService; writes audit entry for each field set. (2) GetCustomerUseCase: Admin/Manager/Agent see any customer; Customer role sees only their own (throws ForbiddenError otherwise); fetches ticket summary from ITicketSummaryService. (3) ListCustomersUseCase: Admin/Manager/Agent; paginated (default 20, max 100); filterable by status; sortable by name/email/createdAt. (4) SearchCustomersUseCase: Admin/Manager/Agent; min 2 chars; searches fullName and email via ILIKE. Unit tests using mocked repositories following 001 patterns. Files in backend/src/application/use-cases/customers/ and backend/tests/unit/use-cases/customers/.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 use cases + tests pass. Roles enforced. Pagination meta returned. 80% branch coverage.
close_reason: All 8 customer use cases implemented with unit tests. 31/31 test suites pass, 312 tests. tsc clean. RBAC enforced in every use case. Audit trail written atomically in UpdateCustomer/UpdateOwnProfile. DeactivateCustomer revokes all tokens.

title: Implement authenticate middleware + auth controller + routes + app wiring
description: Implement: (1) authenticate.middleware.ts — verify Bearer JWT, attach req.user; (2) auth.controller.ts — map all 5 use cases to HTTP; (3) auth.routes.ts — wire middleware+controller; (4) update app.ts to mount /api/v1/auth router. Contract test: tests/contract/auth.routes.test.ts
close_reason: All use cases, middleware, controller, routes, and contract tests implemented. 21/21 test suites pass, 217 tests, 91.6% coverage. Lint clean (0 errors).

title: Implement reset-password.use-case.ts + test
description: Implement ResetPasswordUseCase: validate reset token (not found/used/expired → InvalidResetTokenError), check same-password (SamePasswordError), hash+store new password, revoke all auth tokens, log PASSWORD_RESET_SUCCESS, send password-changed email. Test file: tests/unit/use-cases/reset-password.use-case.test.ts
close_reason: All use cases, middleware, controller, routes, and contract tests implemented. 21/21 test suites pass, 217 tests, 91.6% coverage. Lint clean (0 errors).

title: Implement logout.use-case.ts + test
description: Implement LogoutUseCase: hash raw refresh token, find+revoke it in DB, log LOGOUT event. Idempotent — no error if token not found. Test file: tests/unit/use-cases/logout.use-case.test.ts
close_reason: All use cases, middleware, controller, routes, and contract tests implemented. 21/21 test suites pass, 217 tests, 91.6% coverage. Lint clean (0 errors).

title: Implement refresh-token.use-case.ts + test

close_reason: All use cases, middleware, controller, routes, and contract tests implemented. 21/21 test suites pass, 217 tests, 91.6% coverage. Lint clean (0 errors).

title: Implement forgot-password.use-case.ts + test

close_reason: All use cases, middleware, controller, routes, and contract tests implemented. 21/21 test suites pass, 217 tests, 91.6% coverage. Lint clean (0 errors).

title: User Auth: complete remaining use cases (logout, refresh, forgot-password, reset-password)
description: The login use case is done. This epic covers the remaining 4 application-layer use cases needed to complete the 001-user-auth feature: logout, refresh-token, forgot-password, and reset-password. Each must follow the TDD pattern established by login.use-case.ts and meet 80% coverage.
close_reason: All use cases, middleware, controller, routes, and contract tests implemented. 21/21 test suites pass, 217 tests, 91.6% coverage. Lint clean (0 errors).

title: Add Password value object to domain layer for FR-013 complexity enforcement
description: FR-013 (min 8 chars, >=1 letter, >=1 digit) and FR-014 (reject same password) are domain invariants with no domain-layer home. Without a Password value object, complexity rules are scattered into the application or API layer with no unit-testable domain artifact. Create backend/src/domain/value-objects/password.value-object.ts: immutable class, validates complexity on construction (min 8 chars, regex for letter+digit), throws a new PasswordComplexityError (add to domain.error.ts). FR-014 same-password check requires bcrypt so stays at infrastructure/service layer, but complexity is pure domain.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 1242b10. Adversarial review PASS, 55 tests pass, tsc clean.

title: Write integration tests for PrismaAuthTokenRepository
description: PrismaAuthTokenRepository manages the RefreshToken lifecycle and must guarantee that token rotation and batch revocation are transactionally safe. Tests must cover: create stores tokenHash (SHA-256 of raw token) with correct expiresAt; findByTokenHash returns record or null; revokeByTokenHash sets revokedAt; revokeAllForUser sets revokedAt on every active record for a given userId while leaving already-revoked records unchanged; isValid returns false for revoked tokens, false for expired tokens, true for valid tokens; cascade delete removes tokens when parent User is deleted. Uses real Prisma client with test DB teardown.

title: Write integration tests for PrismaPasswordResetRepository
description: PrismaPasswordResetRepository must enforce the single-active-link invariant (only the most recently issued token is valid). Tests must cover: create inserts a new record with expiresAt = now+1h and usedAt null; invalidatePreviousTokens deletes or nullifies all prior unused tokens for the userId before inserting a new one (FR-011); findByTokenHash returns record or null; markAsUsed sets usedAt to now and subsequent isValid returns false; isValid returns false for expired tokens, false for already-used tokens, true for fresh valid token; cascade delete removes tokens when parent User is deleted. Uses real Prisma client with test DB teardown.

title: Write integration tests for PrismaUserRepository
description: PrismaUserRepository is the persistence boundary for the User entity. Tests must use a real Prisma client pointed at a test database. Cover: findByEmail returns user or null; create stores a lowercase-trimmed email and bcrypt hash; updateFailedAttempts atomically increments count and sets lockedUntil; resetFailedAttempts zeroes the counter and clears lockedUntil; updateStatus transitions ACTIVE to DEACTIVATED; findById returns user or null; email uniqueness constraint raises a typed error. Tests must clean up created rows in afterEach to remain idempotent.

title: Write unit tests for ResetPasswordUseCase
description: ResetPasswordUseCase must enforce all token and password invariants. Tests must cover: valid unused non-expired token + new password meeting complexity rules updates passwordHash, sets usedAt on the token, revokes all active RefreshToken rows for the user, and returns success (FR-012, FR-013); used token (usedAt != null) returns INVALID_RESET_TOKEN (FR-012); expired token (expiresAt < now) returns INVALID_RESET_TOKEN (FR-010); new password identical to current (bcrypt.compare passes) returns VALIDATION_ERROR (FR-014); password failing complexity — <8 chars, no digit, no letter — returns VALIDATION_ERROR (FR-013); success appends PASSWORD_RESET_SUCCESS + PASSWORD_CHANGED and triggers confirmation email (FR-015, FR-017). Repository and bcrypt/email services mocked.

title: Write unit tests for ForgotPasswordUseCase
description: ForgotPasswordUseCase must be timing-safe and prevent user enumeration. Tests must cover: registered email triggers deletion of all prior PasswordResetToken rows for that userId, creation of new PasswordResetToken with expiresAt = now+1h, and dispatch of reset email — response is the identical 200 envelope (FR-009, FR-010, FR-011); unregistered email returns identical 200 envelope without creating any token or sending email (assert email service mock was NOT called); second rapid request for same email invalidates previous token (FR-011); PASSWORD_RESET_REQUEST event appended for registered email, nothing logged that reveals email existence for unknown addresses (FR-017). All mocked.

title: Write unit tests for LogoutUseCase
description: LogoutUseCase must invalidate exactly the presented session and leave all other sessions untouched. Tests must cover: valid access token + matching refreshToken results in revokedAt being set on that RefreshToken record and 204 response (FR-005, SC-003); presenting the same refreshToken a second time after logout is rejected with INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN; a second device's refresh token for the same userId remains active after single-device logout (User Story 3 isolation); missing Authorization header returns 401 UNAUTHORIZED before the use-case executes; LOGOUT event appended to AuthEventLog (FR-017). Prisma and JWT service mocked.

title: Implement AuthController wiring all five use cases to HTTP request/response
description: Translates HTTP requests into use-case calls and maps use-case results or domain errors to the contract-specified HTTP status codes and response envelopes (contracts/auth.md). Methods: login (POST), logout (POST — requires authenticated user from JWT middleware), refresh (POST), forgotPassword (POST), resetPassword (POST). Extract IP address and user-agent from req for event logging. Catch domain errors (InvalidCredentialsError, AccountLockedError, AccountDeactivatedError, InvalidRefreshTokenError, InvalidResetTokenError, SamePasswordError) and map to correct HTTP codes.

title: Implement auth routes and wire into Express application
description: Registers the five POST endpoints under /api/v1/auth with their middleware chains and registers the router in app.ts. Route order: rate-limit middleware -> Zod validation middleware -> controller method. Logout route also requires JWT authentication middleware (verify Bearer token, attach user to req). Route paths: POST /api/v1/auth/login, /api/v1/auth/logout, /api/v1/auth/refresh, /api/v1/auth/forgot-password, /api/v1/auth/reset-password. Register authRoutes in createApp() alongside existing healthRoutes.

title: Implement rate-limit middleware for all auth endpoints
description: Mitigates brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks at the IP level (OWASP A07). Configure express-rate-limit with a tight window for login (10 req / 15 min per IP) and a looser window for other auth endpoints (20 req / 15 min per IP). On limit exceeded, respond 429 with RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED envelope. Export named middleware instances (loginRateLimit, authRateLimit) so routes can apply the correct limiter per endpoint. Apply CORS allowlist configuration from CORS_ORIGINS env var (already in env.ts).
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS (10/10, 1 re-review). Committed 138abb6.

title: Implement request validation middleware using Zod schemas for all auth routes
description: Prevents malformed payloads from reaching use cases and enforces the field rules defined in the API contract. Create a generic validateRequest(schema) middleware factory using zod's safeParse. On failure, respond 422 with VALIDATION_ERROR envelope including per-field detail array. Define and export schemas: LoginSchema (email max 254, password 8-72 chars), RefreshSchema (non-empty string), LogoutSchema (non-empty string), ForgotPasswordSchema (email max 254), ResetPasswordSchema (token non-empty, newPassword 8-72 chars with letter+digit regex). Write unit tests for the middleware and each schema.
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS (10/10, 1 re-review). Committed 138abb6.

title: Implement application-layer DTOs with Zod schemas
description: Create the four DTO files under backend/src/application/dto/: login.dto.ts, refresh-token.dto.ts, forgot-password.dto.ts, and reset-password.dto.ts. Each file exports a Zod schema (for parse-time validation in the presentation layer) and a corresponding inferred TypeScript type. The login DTO must require a valid email and a non-empty password string. The reset-password DTO must enforce minimum password length matching the spec (>=8 chars, >=1 letter, >=1 digit). These DTOs are the sole validated input shape for every use case and ensure no invalid data crosses the presentation-to-application boundary.
close_reason: 4-phase loop complete. Adversarial review PASS (8/8). Committed f1eb9c1.

title: Write success criteria validation plan with owners and timing
description: SC-001 through SC-008 define measurable thresholds (e.g., login flow under 30s, password reset email within 2 minutes, logout invalidation within 5 seconds, 100% audit log persistence) but the spec contains no plan for when and how these will be verified, who owns each measurement, and what tooling will be used. A PM-owned validation plan must specify: the verification method for each SC (automated test, manual QA run, load test, email delivery probe), the environment in which it will be measured, and the point in the release cycle at which it must pass.

title: Create AC sign-off checklist for each user story
description: The existing requirements.md checklist covers spec quality, not QA handoff readiness. A PM-owned acceptance criteria sign-off checklist is needed for each of the four user stories (Secure Login, Session Continuity, Secure Logout, Forgotten Password Recovery) so that QA and the feature owner have a shared gate before the feature is marked done. Each story's AC scenarios from the spec must be listed as individually verifiable checkboxes, including all negative/lockout/enumeration-prevention paths.

title: Implement RefreshTokenUseCase with rolling rotation and token reuse detection
description: Enables silent session renewal without repeated password entry (P2 — FR-003, FR-004). Hash the incoming opaque token and look it up; reject if not found, revokedAt IS NOT NULL, or expiresAt < now with 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN. Within a single transaction: revoke the old token (set revokedAt), create a new opaque token + hash (7-day TTL), and sign a new 15-min JWT. Write TOKEN_REFRESH to AuthEventLog. Return new token pair in the contract envelope.

title: Clarify ownership and timeline for WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility requirement
description: The spec's Assumptions section states that accessibility requirements (screen reader support, keyboard navigation on the login form) are in scope and must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards. The implementation plan is explicitly backend-only, and the React Native frontend is deferred to a separate workspace. As written, the accessibility requirement has no owner, no implementation plan, and no validation milestone. The PM must either formally defer this requirement to the frontend feature track with a linked dependency, or remove it from the 001-user-auth scope.

title: Implement Authentication Module
Implement LogoutUseCase to invalidate the specific refresh token
description: Allows users to end a session explicitly and prevents refresh token reuse after logout (P3 — FR-005). Accept the raw refresh token from the request body; SHA-256 hash it; set revokedAt = now on the matching RefreshToken row (no-op if not found — idempotent). Write LOGOUT to AuthEventLog keyed to the authenticated user (userId from the verified access token). Respond 204 No Content. Per-device isolation: only the presented token is revoked; other device sessions remain active (User Story 3, SC-003).

title: Implement ForgotPasswordUseCase with enumeration-safe email dispatch
description: Triggers the password-reset flow without revealing whether an email is registered (P4 — FR-009, FR-010, FR-011). Normalize and look up the email; if not found, return the same 200 response (no branch timing leak — always run the same code path). If found: delete all existing PasswordResetToken rows for userId (single-use invariant, FR-011), generate a cryptographically random opaque token, store SHA-256 hash with 1-hour TTL, send the reset-link email via NodemailerEmailService, and write PASSWORD_RESET_REQUEST to AuthEventLog. Always respond 200 with the generic acknowledgment message.

title: Implement ResetPasswordUseCase with complexity validation and forced session revocation
description: Completes the password-reset flow, enforcing security invariants before committing the change (P4 — FR-012 through FR-016). Hash the incoming token and look it up; reject (400 INVALID_RESET_TOKEN) if not found, usedAt IS NOT NULL, or expiresAt < now. Validate newPassword complexity: >=8 chars, >=1 letter, >=1 digit, <=72 chars; reject 422 on failure. Compare new password against current passwordHash with bcrypt; reject if same (FR-014). Hash new password (bcrypt cost >=12); update User.passwordHash; mark PasswordResetToken.usedAt = now; revoke ALL active RefreshTokens for userId (force re-login across all devices); send confirmation email (FR-015); write PASSWORD_RESET_SUCCESS + PASSWORD_CHANGED to AuthEventLog (FR-017).
